description: LogPeck Master Diagnostic Rules - Expert Forensic Edition

rules.label: 🚨 COLLSCAN (Missing Index)
rules.description: Targeted collection has no index for this query shape. MongoDB must scan every document to satisfy the result set, causing massive CPU and I/O overhead.

rules.condition: SORT in plan_summary and IXSCAN not in plan_summary
rules.label: 🚨 In-Memory Sort
rules.description: Result set was sorted in memory without the help of an index. This is extremely slow for large datasets and carries a high risk of 'Sort exceeded memory limit' errors.

rules.label: ⚠️ High Planning ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: The query optimizer is spending a disproportionate amount of time choosing an execution plan. Often caused by index complexity, high plan cache churn, or lack of hint usage.

rules.label: ⚠️ I/O Bound ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: Operation spent over 30% of its time waiting for disk. This indicates heavy physical read/write activity, possible disk throttling, or a 'cold cache' situation.

rules.label: ⚠️ Inefficient Index (Ratio: {value:.0f}:1)
rules.description: The index used for this query is not selective enough. MongoDB is scanning many index keys to return very few documents, indicating suboptimal index field ordering.

rules.label: 🚨 High Yields ({value:.0f}/op)
rules.description: The operation yielded its lock frequently to other threads. High yield counts often follow high I/O wait times or indicate extreme concurrency stress on the cluster.

rules.label: 🚨 Latency Cliff ({value:.1f}x Avg)
rules.description: Extreme performance variance detected. The slowest sample is significantly slower than the average, indicating intermittent blocking, resource spikes, or lock queuing.

rules.label: 🚨 Write Conflicts
rules.description: Optimistic concurrency failure. Multiple operations attempted to write to the same document or range simultaneously, forcing the database to retry the operation.

rules.label: 🚨 CPU Bound ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: The operation spent nearly all its time in compute. This is typical for complex aggregations, heavy data encoding/decoding, or cryptographic operations.

rules.label: 📉 REDUCTION PIPELINE (Ratio: {value:.0f}:1)
rules.description: Stage-heavy aggregation that examines many documents in early stages before collapsing into few results. Often indicates a missing or late $match stage.

rules.label: 🐌 IDLE TRANSACTION
rules.description: The multi-statement transaction was open but inactive for more than 50% of its duration. This usually indicates the transaction is waiting on external application logic.

rules.label: 🔗 ACTIVE TRANSACTION
rules.description: Multi-document transaction identified. These operations carry higher overhead and lock pressure than standard CRUD.

rules.label: 🚨 OPLOG WAIT ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: Concurrency bottleneck during write operations. Indicates high parallel write pressure or slow oplog application on secondary nodes.

rules.label: 🚨 EXECUTION QUEUE ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: Significant ticket queueing detected. The query spent most of its life waiting for an available execution slot, indicating the cluster has reached its concurrency limit.

rules.label: 🚨 TIMEOUT
rules.description: Operation was terminated by the server because it exceeded its 'maxTimeMS' limit. Results are likely incomplete or failed entirely.

rules.label: 🚨 SEARCH BOTTLENECK ({value:.0f}%)
rules.description: Atlas Search bottleneck. The database engine spent over 70% of its time waiting for results from the 'mongot' search process.

rules.label: 🚨 REGEX SCAN
rules.description: The query uses a regular expression filter. Regex matches, especially those with the 'i' (case-insensitive) flag or non-anchored patterns, are CPU-intensive and cannot always fully leverage indexes.
